name: Day 10: we’re so back
description: Woke up after the Worst Night Of All Time™ to a foggy cold day for the first 8 miles. Fog burned off and the skies cleared for the most incredible views of the trail so far, plus some trail magic from Pastor Steve and a stop for the night at the NOC!

name: Day 9: the one where everything went wrong
description: Holy hard day. Made a quick town stop into Franklin, NC for a resupply and my first vegetable of the trail in an attempt to beat scurvy. And thennn the day went so downhill after that! Torrential downpour, gunshots (?), lightning and thunder, and 12 miles to an absolutely packed campsite where every attempt to dry my clothes was unsuccessful. Also, I fell off a log mid-pee and almost fell off a cliff. Sweet!

name: Day 8: 100 miles complete!
description: Almost my first 20 miler! Easy-ish terrain until a crazy climb up to the fire tower on Albert Mountain that marks the first 100 miles of the Appalachian Trail. Hiking felt so good today and the weather was gorgeous but we’re about to pay the price in the rain tomorrow!

name: Day 7: see ya GA, hello NC!
description: Had the butterflies for company today on a long solo 18 miles! Took me on a blue blaze to the wreckage of a plane crash, and finally met back up with Charlotte and Sam at Standing Indian for an incredible sunset and a great tent city!

name: Day 6: last full day in GA!
description:
Slackpacked the 17 miles between Unicoi Gap and Dick’s Creek Gap and felt light as a feather and even got a little running in there! It’s just hard to watch the miles go by so slowly when I’m used to clicking off a mile in less than 10 minutes and now they take 20-30. Especially when I’m hiking alone! But I’m back at Stanimals for one more night and then I’ll cross over to North Carolina tomorrow!

End mile: 69.3

name: Day 5
description: 10 miles done and dusted before noon! First hostel stay tonight and I have never been more excited for a shower, a frozen pizza, and the Celtics

name: Day 4
description:
Long day! Saw the shoe tree at Neels Gap (where people who quit the trail on day 3 throw their shoes up), did a mini resupply at Mountain Crossings at Walasi-Yi, and rejoined my new friends from yesterday back at Low Gap Shelter!

End mile: 43

name: Day 3
description:
Survived a scary thunderstorm in my tent last night and was rewarded with low humidity, stunning views, and some new friends today!

End mile: 27.7

name: Day 2
description:
This is hard man

End mile: 13.6

name: Day 1
description:
The approach trail to the start of the Appalachian Trail being 8 miles is actually incredibly on brand

End mile: 1

name: Bittersweet!
description: One last Sunny Girl with the Sunny King
